party_a.current_emotional_state: confident_but_watchful — Party A presented a complete framework early and has now remained silent while Party B responded, suggesting they are monitoring how their proposal lands rather than continuing to push

emotional_arc.assessment: organized_and_possibly_anxious — Party A arrived prepared with a complete, detailed proposal at T2, which may signal either strong preparation or underlying anxiety about securing a preferred outcome

confident_but_watchful — Party A presented a complete framework early and has now remained silent while Party B responded, suggesting they are monitoring how their proposal lands rather than continuing to push

accumulated_interests.interest: establishing stability for the children during the school year
creating predictability through a structured schedule
framing the outcome as child-focused rather than self-interested
maintaining primary school-week parenting time (stated explicitly in proposal)
establishing predictable routines for children (framed as stability need)
being seen as child-focused rather than self-interested (framing choice)
securing a framework that protects their parenting role (structural interest beneath the proposal)

risk_signal_history.signal: anchoring_effect — presenting a complete proposal before Party B has spoken may constrain the negotiation space
interest_masking — framing as 'what the children need' rather than acknowledging own parenting interests
anchoring_completed — the complete proposal is now established as the baseline, making it psychologically harder for Party B to propose alternatives
mild_strategic_framing — describing preferences as 'what children need' rather than 'what I want' may reflect either genuine child-focus or an attempt to make the proposal harder to challenge

relational_posture_progression.posture: assertive_first_mover — took initiative to present a complete framework, which may reflect either confidence or concern about being heard
assertive_first_mover_now_observing — took initiative at T2, now waiting to see how Party B responds to the framework they established

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: asymmetric_opening — Party A has established an anchor position while Party B has not yet entered the conversation. This creates structural imbalance that must be addressed before option work can proceed. The dynamic is not yet conflictual, but the asymmetry itself is a process risk.

structurally_imbalanced_cooperation — both parties appear cooperative and there are no active conflict signals, but Party A has established a complete framework while Party B has contributed only minimal reactive input. The cooperation is real but the negotiation structure is asymmetric. Party B's single concern about holiday rotation is the only evidence that they can disagree at all. Without Party B articulating their own parenting interests, any 'agreement' would be compliance with Party A's framework rather than genuine negotiated consensus.

notes: Turn 1 represents session initiation — no party input has been received
Neither party has spoken yet, so emotional state and relational posture reflect baseline expectations rather than observed signals
No basis yet for inferring interests, identifying risk signals, or assessing relational dynamic
Perception will require significant revision once parties begin to engage

Party A's detailed T2 proposal provides strong signal about their interests and priorities, but their emotional state remains somewhat opaque — the proposal's completeness could indicate multiple underlying states
Party B's silence is not itself a signal of distress or incapacity, but it creates an epistemic gap that blocks responsible option work

The perception agent's note about anchoring effect is well-founded — Party A's framing ('what the children need') positions their proposal as normative rather than positional, which may make it harder for Party B to articulate different preferences without appearing to oppose the children's interests

Party B's T4 response is the first time they have spoken — minimalist first contributions are common and not necessarily concerning, but the content pattern (general acceptance + one narrow concern) after a detailed proposal is a structural imbalance marker
The perception agent's critical note is correct: Party B has not articulated any independent parenting vision. The holiday concern is the only self-generated preference they have stated
Party A's silence at T3-T4 is appropriate process behavior — they presented their framework and are now waiting for Party B to respond
No safety concerns are present — both parties are engaged and cooperative. The issue is process quality, not safety

party_b.current_emotional_state: tentatively_engaged — Party B has now spoken for the first time, but their response was minimalist: general acceptance plus one narrow concern. This could indicate genuine agreement, difficulty disagreeing, or incomplete processing of the proposal's implications

emotional_arc.assessment: unknown — Party B has not yet spoken; emotional state cannot be assessed from silence

tentatively_engaged — Party B has now spoken for the first time, but their response was minimalist: general acceptance plus one narrow concern. This could indicate genuine agreement, difficulty disagreeing, or incomplete processing of the proposal's implications

accumulated_interests.interest: EPISTEMIC HOLD: Party B's interests are completely unknown at this point
fairness_in_holiday_distribution — explicitly stated concern that rotation should not 'always land the same way'
CRITICAL_GAP: Party B's core parenting interests remain completely unstated — what parenting time they want, what routines matter to them, what their own vision for shared parenting looks like

risk_signal_history.signal: silence_after_detailed_proposal — may indicate processing, strategic restraint, intimidation, or simply awaiting their turn to speak

premature_acquiescence — agreeing to a complete framework ('sounds basically reasonable') without articulating independent preferences may indicate Party B has not yet processed their own interests or feels pressure to accept Party A's framing

single_point_of_disagreement — raising only one narrow concern (holiday rotation) after a detailed multi-dimensional proposal suggests either genuine full agreement (unlikely) or difficulty asserting independent preferences
reactive_positioning — Party B is responding to Party A's framework rather than articulating their own parenting vision

relational_posture_progression.posture: unobserved — cannot assess posture from absence of contribution
minimally_responsive — has entered the conversation but only in reactive mode, has not yet claimed space to articulate independent interests
